sequences.name: Recommended Latin Lowercase Build Order
sequences.description: Evidence-based sequence for building a Latin lowercase efficiently, minimizing rework.

stages.name: Anchors
stages.rationale: o establishes counter shape and spacing unit. n establishes stem weight, arch, and serves as the arch template.
stages.completion_criteria: o and n spaced and looking balanced together in 'nonn' string

stages.name: DNA Expansion — Reading Method
stages.rationale: University of Reading method. Provides enough variety to test texture and reveal systemic issues before committing to the full set.

alternatives.name: Type Together method
alternatives.rationale: Preferred when diagonal variety is important early

stages.completion_criteria: Can set real words with available letters; spacing feels consistent

stages.name: Arch Family
stages.rationale: All derive directly from n. Should be fast to build.
stages.completion_criteria: All consistent with n in arch weight and spacing

stages.name: Bowl Family
stages.rationale: All derive from d (which derives from o + h). Should be fast.
stages.completion_criteria: All consistent with d in bowl shape

stages.name: Diagonal & Difficult
stages.rationale: Letters without close structural relatives. Each needs fresh drawing.
stages.completion_criteria: All 26 lowercase letters complete and consistently spaced

stages.name: Capitals
stages.rationale: H and O establish cap-to-lowercase proportion. Then high-frequency and structurally-generative letters. Then remainder.

stages.name: Figures
stages.rationale: zero establishes figure oval; one establishes figure stem. Same DNA approach as letterforms.
stages.completion_criteria: All 10 figures consistent in weight and spacing
